topic: SAA African cell field intensity below 21,795 nT
our_argument_summary: Our review says both WMM2025 and the dome model extrapolate the same INTERMAGNET station data for the SAA African cell decay trend (~25 nT/yr). The dome adds no unique prediction — no mechanism for *why* the rate is ~25 nT/yr, no prediction of when it reverses, and no model of internal SAA structure. Verdict: 'Std Model Explains' — non-discriminating trend extrapolation.

relevant_files: monitor.py — Domain 8: 'SAA Decay Rate (TTB)' monitors Tsumeb station for >=28 nT/yr decay
inject_ai_layer.py — hardcoded WIN-035 metadata with static 'confirmed' status
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: WIN-035 has NO dedicated monitoring domain in monitor.py. The closest domain is Domain 8 ('SAA Decay Rate TTB'), which monitors general SAA decay at the Tsumeb (TTB) station against a threshold of >=28 nT/yr. This domain does NOT separately track the African cell minimum intensity against the 21,795 nT threshold. WIN-035 appears in inject_ai_layer.py's confirmed_wins array as a static entry (W009 mapping). The 'confirmation' was done by manually comparing NOAA WMM2025 output to the threshold, not by automated monitoring.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Hardcoded. The threshold 21,795 nT is derived from WIN-005's observed decay curve (23,050 → 21,880 nT from CHAOS-7), extrapolated forward. This is a trend-following calculation, not a dome-geometry derivation. The inject_ai_layer.py file stores the result as static metadata. No code computes the African cell minimum dynamically.
data_sources_used: NOAA WMM2025 (claimed confirmation source — ~30 nT drop since January 2025)
CHAOS-7 (historical data for WIN-005 baseline: 23,050 → 21,880 nT)
INTERMAGNET Tsumeb (TTB) station (generic SAA decay monitoring)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: If the African cell intensity stabilized or reversed, the dome could invoke 'aetheric damping equilibrium' or redefine the measurement window. The 6-day gap between registration (2026-03-06) and confirmation (2026-03-12) suggests the data was already known when the prediction was registered.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None significant. Our review correctly identifies this as non-discriminating trend extrapolation. One minor point: our review says '~25 nT/yr' but the dome's own data and WIN-037 both cite '~30 nT' drop and '>=28 nT/year' threshold. The actual African cell decay rate may be closer to 28-30 nT/yr at the cell minimum, not 25. This doesn't change the argument but could be tightened for precision.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: monitor.py Domain 8 monitors generic SAA decay rate at Tsumeb (TTB) station against >=28 nT/yr threshold, but does NOT track African cell minimum intensity against the 21,795 nT threshold specific to WIN-035. No domain in monitor.py references 21,795 nT or the African cell specifically. The WIN-035 confirmation was manual, not automated. Tagging as 'none' for WIN-035 specifically (the related Domain 8 covers WIN-037/042's general decay rate, not this WIN's specific threshold).
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: The SAA African cell decay has been tracked by NOAA, ESA/Swarm, and CHAOS-7 models for years. WMM2025 (updated annually) provides the same field intensity data. The dome model's 'prediction' uses the same observational data and the same extrapolation method as standard geomagnetic monitoring. The dome relabels the cause as 'aetheric field geometry' instead of 'reversed-flux patches at core-mantle boundary' but predicts the identical numerical trend.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: PROS-005 was registered 2026-03-06 and confirmed 2026-03-12 — a 6-day turnaround. The threshold 21,795 nT was derived from WIN-005's CHAOS-7 data (African cell: 23,050 → 21,880 nT over 2000-2025). This is retroactive curve extrapolation: the decay trend was already well-established from 25 years of data. WMM2025 was already published when the prediction was registered. The 'prediction' is reading a known trend forward by a trivial amount.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: The 21,795 nT threshold is stated as 'derived from the WIN-005 exponential decay curve extrapolation.' WIN-005's data comes from CHAOS-7 observations (23,050 → 21,880 nT). The extrapolation uses the observed decay rate, not any dome geometric parameters (disc_radius, firmament_height, etc.). No formula in the dome's repository starts from dome geometry and arrives at 21,795 nT. This is observational curve-fitting, not geometric derivation.

holes_found.description: Decay rate discrepancy: our review says '~25 nT/yr' but the dome's own data and related WINs (037, 042) cite '~30 nT' drop and '>=28 nT/year'. The actual African cell decay rate at the minimum appears to be 28-30 nT/yr based on the dome's own sources. Our lower figure slightly understates the trend.
holes_found.recommendation: Update to '~28-30 nT/yr' or cite both the local cell minimum rate and the broader regional average if they differ. Cross-reference with WIN-037 and WIN-042 for consistency.

holes_found.description: Our review misses the most damning fact: PROS-005 was registered on 2026-03-06 and confirmed on 2026-03-12 — only 6 days later. This near-instant confirmation strongly suggests the confirming data (WMM2025) was already available when the 'prediction' was registered. A genuine prospective prediction would have a meaningful lead time before confirmation. This is the strongest evidence that WIN-035 is post-hoc retrodiction disguised as prediction.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a sentence noting the 6-day registration-to-confirmation gap as evidence that the data was already in hand. Example: 'PROS-005 was registered 6 March 2026 and confirmed 12 March 2026 — a 6-day turnaround that suggests the confirming WMM2025 data was already available at registration time, making this retrodiction rather than prediction.'

holes_found.description: Our review says the dome 'offers no mechanism for why the decay rate is ~25 nT/yr rather than any other value.' This is accurate but could be stronger. The dome actually DOES claim a mechanism — 'aetheric field geometry predicts the African cell decays faster because of its position relative to the pole axis.' We should explicitly engage with this claimed mechanism and show it is empty (no equations, no derivation from dome parameters).
holes_found.recommendation: Add: 'The dome claims "aetheric field geometry" explains faster African cell decay due to position relative to the pole axis, but provides no equations connecting disc_radius (20,015 km) or firmament parameters to the specific 28-30 nT/yr rate. The mechanism is asserted, not derived.'

holes_found.description: The detail_evidence mentions INTERMAGNET as a data source alongside WMM2025, but doesn't clarify the relationship. WMM2025 is a model derived partly from INTERMAGNET data. Listing them separately could confuse readers into thinking they are independent confirmations.
holes_found.recommendation: Clarify: 'WMM2025, which incorporates INTERMAGNET observatory data' rather than listing them as separate sources.

stronger_arguments.description: The 6-day prediction-to-confirmation window is devastating. No scientific prediction registered 6 days before confirmation counts as prospective. WMM2025 data was publicly available well before March 2026. The dome author knew the answer before posting the question.

The threshold 21,795 nT is suspiciously precise for what is essentially 'the trend will continue.' It's derived by extrapolating WIN-005's decay curve (23,050 → 21,880 nT over ~25 years). Given the ~28-30 nT/yr rate, crossing any threshold set slightly below the last measured value is virtually guaranteed within a year. The prediction has near-zero information content — it's equivalent to predicting 'tomorrow will be warmer than yesterday' during a heat wave.
sources: WIN-005 CHAOS-7 data: African cell 23,050 → 21,880 nT (2000-2025)

stronger_arguments.description: A dome defender might argue: 'But we DID predict the threshold from our model's decay curve, and it was confirmed.' The rebuttal is that any observer with a ruler and the CHAOS-7 dataset could make the same prediction. The WMM2025 model itself predicts this. The National Centers for Environmental Information (NCEI) publishes field-strength forecasts. This is like 'predicting' the next Schumann resonance measurement will be ~7.83 Hz — it's reading a well-known stable value, not testing a model.

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome model correctly identifies that the SAA African cell is decaying faster than the American cell, and that the field intensity has dropped to historically unprecedented lows. This is a real and scientifically significant observation. The dome also correctly notes that the decay is ongoing and measurable at specific stations. The recognition of asymmetric SAA behavior is genuine scientific awareness.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Recognizing an observation is not the same as predicting it. The dome did not derive the 21,795 nT threshold from its geometry — it extrapolated from the same CHAOS-7 data that standard models use. The 6-day registration-to-confirmation gap confirms this was post-hoc. Any model that reads CHAOS-7 data and draws a line forward would arrive at the same 'prediction.' Standard geomagnetic models (WMM, IGRF) already do this routinely and with quantified uncertainty bounds, which the dome model lacks.

deeper_analysis:
WIN-035 is one of the clearest examples of the dome model's 'prediction laundering' pattern: take a well-established observational trend, register it as a 'prospective prediction' with a precise threshold derived from that same trend, then 'confirm' it days later using publicly available data.

The specific mechanism:
1. WIN-005 established (from CHAOS-7) that the African SAA cell decayed from 23,050 to 21,880 nT over ~25 years.
2. The dome extrapolated this trend forward and set a threshold of 21,795 nT (just 85 nT below the last measured value).
3. At ~28-30 nT/yr decay rate, this threshold would be crossed within ~3 months of the last measurement.
4. PROS-005 was registered when WMM2025 data was already available, then 'confirmed' 6 days later.

This is not prediction — it is retroactive trend-following with a cosmetic time lag. The information content of the prediction is near zero: given the trend, the probability of crossing 21,795 nT within a year was >99%.

Our current verdict ('Std Model Explains') is correct. The argument could be strengthened by:
1. Adding the 6-day turnaround as explicit evidence of post-hoc timing.
2. Correcting '~25 nT/yr' to '~28-30 nT/yr' for consistency with the dome's own claimed rates.
3. Noting that WMM2025 itself is a forward-looking model that already predicts this same trend with quantified uncertainties.
4. Making explicit that the threshold (21,795 nT) has no derivation from dome geometry — it's WIN-005's observed data extrapolated.

The dome defender's strongest counter would be: 'We registered the prediction before confirming data, which makes it prospective.' The rebuttal is that (a) the data was already available (WMM2025 published months earlier), (b) the 6-day gap is suspicious, and (c) even if genuinely prospective, extrapolating a 25-year linear trend by 3 months is trivial, not discriminating.

Confidence 0.95: very high that the verdict holds. The 5% uncertainty reflects the possibility that the dome could produce a derivation showing why 21,795 nT specifically (not 21,800 or 21,790) follows from dome geometry — but no such derivation exists in the repository.

citations_verified: WMM2025 link (https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/products/world-magnetic-model) — verified as correct NCEI page for WMM
INTERMAGNET link (https://www.intermagnet.org/) — verified as correct INTERMAGNET portal
citations_unchecked: No DOIs cited in WIN-035 detail text — only two URL links, both verified above
